step_by_step_thinking:
tRNA does not bind directly to mRNA in prokaryotic translation initiation. The Shine-Dalgarno sequence plays a role in initiating translation in bacteria by aligning the ribosomal RNA with the start codon of the mRNA. Capping and poly-A tails are involved in processing eukaryotic mRNAs rather than prokaryotic ones. Therefore:
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about bacterial translation mechanisms, only the Shine-Dalgarno sequence facilitates binding of the ribosome to the mRNA during initiation of translation in prokaryotes. Thus,
raw_response:
tRNA does not bind directly to mRNA in prokaryotic translation initiation. The Shine-Dalgarno sequence plays a role in initiating translation in bacteria by aligning the ribosomal RNA with the start codon of the mRNA. Capping and poly-A tails are involved in processing eukaryotic mRNAs rather than prokaryotic ones. Therefore:
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about bacterial translation mechanisms, only the Shine-Dalgarno sequence facilitates binding of the ribosome to the mRNA during initiation of translation in prokaryotes. Thus, B is correct.
